items.subject: giving you the opportunity
items.body_text:
[Ian Rae]
...to ask your team and colleagues for:

Server inventory (ideally showing which assets are relevant /useful for Aptum IaaS) - Matt Carter is where I usually go

DC capacity (where do we have space and power where we can deploy the IaaS) - George

APT cloud roadmap - where will the IaaS be rolling out and when (current plan) and where are we considering it in the future. When are old Aptum Cloud and MTC clients being migrated? - Martin

Bonus question - related roadmap for APT Cloud Azure and AWS customers. - Will
As Dave will be pressing on these questions.

I'm sure none of this surprises you since...well, it is already in progress. But Dave will see it as his initiative so think about how to coordinate this and follow up with him please! 

Have a smooth trip back

Thanks

ian

Ian Rae
President & CEO
+1 (514) 944-4008
aptum.com | LinkedIn

items.subject: Let's talk about what's next for your services org
items.body_text:
[George Humphrey | TSIA]
No pitch. Just a conversation about what's shifting in 2026.
 

Connect quote-to-delivery with AI and automation. 
 View this email in your browser 

Hi Adam,

AI Economics™ is forcing a fundamental shift in how tech services organizations price, deliver, and prove value. The gap between what needs to change and what's actually blocking progress is where most leaders get stuck.

So here's the question: what's actually blocking you right now?
Most tech services leaders we talk to are facing some version of the same tension: high awareness of what needs to shift, but low clarity on where to start. If that sounds familiar, let’s talk.  

No deck. No pitch. Just a straightforward conversation about what you’re navigating and where TSIA research might help you move faster.

Book a call with us 

Best,
George Humphrey
Senior Vice President of Research

            

This email was sent by: TSIA
17065 Camino San Bernardo, Suite 150 San Diego, CA, 92127, US

Subject: Centrilogic & Aptum - Meeting Minutes – Bracknell update & Strategy - 26-03-30
 

Overview of Customer Footprint
The majority of customers are hosted in standard Centrilogic cabinets using shared power and carrier infrastructure.
One customer operates IBM mainframe equipment housed in non‑standard cabinets (wider and deeper), requiring confirmation of:
Power requirements
Weight and handling constraints
Only one customer (Perforce) operates within a dedicated cage (three cabinets plus expansion capacity).
Several customers also occupy office space in addition to data centre cabinets, which may influence their migration decisions.
 
Customer‑Specific Observations
Digital AI
Uses Centrilogic cabinets.
Requires physical separation between cabinets due to Wi‑Fi interference constraints.
Cloudian
Operates three cabinets with relatively high power consumption.
Also uses office space, though this is primarily for convenience.
It was agreed that, if migrating, the focus would likely be on rack space only, not office space.
Infinity
Uses one cabinet and office space.
Staff are onsite daily, making a migration to Portsmouth potentially less attractive.
OpenText
Uses six cabinets (five active, one for storage).
Makes frequent use of Smart Hands, which is included in their contract.
Perforce
Only cage customer at Bracknell.
Likely to seek an alternative location closer to their existing office, though requirements will be clarified in case migration is considered.
Telco providers (e.g., CityFibre, Colt)
Not expected to migrate to Aptum.
Will require time to remove or migrate their downstream customers from Bracknell.
 
Likelihood of Migration to Aptum
The general assessment is that more than 50% of customers may choose to migrate to Aptum.
A key differentiator will be the availability of migration assistance services, including:
De‑racking
Transportation
Re‑racking
Optional re‑cabling and Smart Hands support
Providing structured migration support is expected to significantly increase customer uptake.
 
Information Required to Progress

Aptum requested that the customer tracking spreadsheet include the following information for each customer: Number of cabinets and approximate REUs
Power provisioned and, where available, estimated power utilisation
Power feeds (A/B), number of circuits
Connectivity details:
Port speeds (1G, 10G)
Bandwidth utilisation
Use of Centrilogic carriers vs. customer‑owned circuits
IP address information:
IP blocks assigned (e.g. /29)
Confirmation of inclusion within the proposed /24 block
Contact status:
Whether the customer has already been contacted
By whom and when

 

Connectivity and IP Considerations
Most customers rely on Centrilogic carrier mix (Telia, LINX, Cogent, Virgin, Vodafone).
A small number of customers use customer‑owned last‑mile circuits (primarily BT or Virgin).
No major technical blockers were identified:
Aptum has sufficient space and power capacity.
Carrier compatibility appears feasible.
Follow‑up is required to:
Confirm power requirements for the IBM mainframe equipment.
Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility for migrating customers.
Confirm treatment of the /24 IP block for customer continuity.
 
Customer Communications
Two draft customer notification letters have been prepared but have not yet been sent.
Formal communication is intentionally being held until landlord agreements are finalised.
It was agreed that:
Aptum should not contact customers directly until a formal introduction is made by Centrilogic.
The draft letter will be shared with Aptum for review (already shared).
Aptum will contribute language describing:
Facility capabilities
Migration assistance services
The previously referenced June 30 date is not yet firm and remains subject to landlord agreement.
 
Key Agreements
No critical technical blockers were identified for the majority of customers.
Migration assistance is essential to the success of customer transitions.
The enriched customer spreadsheet is the critical dependency for next steps.
Early and coordinated communication will be key once formal notices are issued.

Share the spreadsheet and draft customer letter in the agreed shared folder. DONE
Aptum
Review the draft customer communication.

Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility.
All parties
Re‑engage once landlord agreements allow formal customer communication.

Subject: Centrilogic & Aptum - Meeting Minutes – Bracknell update & Strategy - 26-03-30
 

Overview of Customer Footprint
The majority of customers are hosted in standard Centrilogic cabinets using shared power and carrier infrastructure.
One customer operates IBM mainframe equipment housed in non‑standard cabinets (wider and deeper), requiring confirmation of:
Power requirements
Weight and handling constraints
Only one customer (Perforce) operates within a dedicated cage (three cabinets plus expansion capacity).
Several customers also occupy office space in addition to data centre cabinets, which may influence their migration decisions.
 
Customer‑Specific Observations
Digital AI
Uses Centrilogic cabinets.
Requires physical separation between cabinets due to Wi‑Fi interference constraints.
Cloudian
Operates three cabinets with relatively high power consumption.
Also uses office space, though this is primarily for convenience.
It was agreed that, if migrating, the focus would likely be on rack space only, not office space.
Infinity
Uses one cabinet and office space.
Staff are onsite daily, making a migration to Portsmouth potentially less attractive.
OpenText
Uses six cabinets (five active, one for storage).
Makes frequent use of Smart Hands, which is included in their contract.
Perforce
Only cage customer at Bracknell.
Likely to seek an alternative location closer to their existing office, though requirements will be clarified in case migration is considered.
Telco providers (e.g., CityFibre, Colt)
Not expected to migrate to Aptum.
Will require time to remove or migrate their downstream customers from Bracknell.
 
Likelihood of Migration to Aptum
The general assessment is that more than 50% of customers may choose to migrate to Aptum.
A key differentiator will be the availability of migration assistance services, including:
De‑racking
Transportation
Re‑racking
Optional re‑cabling and Smart Hands support
Providing structured migration support is expected to significantly increase customer uptake.
 
Information Required to Progress

Aptum requested that the customer tracking spreadsheet include the following information for each customer: Number of cabinets and approximate REUs
Power provisioned and, where available, estimated power utilisation
Power feeds (A/B), number of circuits
Connectivity details:
Port speeds (1G, 10G)
Bandwidth utilisation
Use of Centrilogic carriers vs. customer‑owned circuits
IP address information:
IP blocks assigned (e.g. /29)
Confirmation of inclusion within the proposed /24 block
Contact status:
Whether the customer has already been contacted
By whom and when

 

Connectivity and IP Considerations
Most customers rely on Centrilogic carrier mix (Telia, LINX, Cogent, Virgin, Vodafone).
A small number of customers use customer‑owned last‑mile circuits (primarily BT or Virgin).
No major technical blockers were identified:
Aptum has sufficient space and power capacity.
Carrier compatibility appears feasible.
Follow‑up is required to:
Confirm power requirements for the IBM mainframe equipment.
Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility for migrating customers.
Confirm treatment of the /24 IP block for customer continuity.
 
Customer Communications
Two draft customer notification letters have been prepared but have not yet been sent.
Formal communication is intentionally being held until landlord agreements are finalised.
It was agreed that:
Aptum should not contact customers directly until a formal introduction is made by Centrilogic.
The draft letter will be shared with Aptum for review (already shared).
Aptum will contribute language describing:
Facility capabilities
Migration assistance services
The previously referenced June 30 date is not yet firm and remains subject to landlord agreement.
 
Key Agreements
No critical technical blockers were identified for the majority of customers.
Migration assistance is essential to the success of customer transitions.
The enriched customer spreadsheet is the critical dependency for next steps.
Early and coordinated communication will be key once formal notices are issued.

Subject: Centrilogic & Aptum - Meeting Minutes – Bracknell update & Strategy - 26-03-30
 

Overview of Customer Footprint
The majority of customers are hosted in standard Centrilogic cabinets using shared power and carrier infrastructure.
One customer operates IBM mainframe equipment housed in non‑standard cabinets (wider and deeper), requiring confirmation of:
Power requirements
Weight and handling constraints
Only one customer (Perforce) operates within a dedicated cage (three cabinets plus expansion capacity).
Several customers also occupy office space in addition to data centre cabinets, which may influence their migration decisions.
 
Customer‑Specific Observations
Digital AI
Uses Centrilogic cabinets.
Requires physical separation between cabinets due to Wi‑Fi interference constraints.
Cloudian
Operates three cabinets with relatively high power consumption.
Also uses office space, though this is primarily for convenience.
It was agreed that, if migrating, the focus would likely be on rack space only, not office space.
Infinity
Uses one cabinet and office space.
Staff are onsite daily, making a migration to Portsmouth potentially less attractive.
OpenText
Uses six cabinets (five active, one for storage).
Makes frequent use of Smart Hands, which is included in their contract.
Perforce
Only cage customer at Bracknell.
Likely to seek an alternative location closer to their existing office, though requirements will be clarified in case migration is considered.
Telco providers (e.g., CityFibre, Colt)
Not expected to migrate to Aptum.
Will require time to remove or migrate their downstream customers from Bracknell.
 
Likelihood of Migration to Aptum
The general assessment is that more than 50% of customers may choose to migrate to Aptum.
A key differentiator will be the availability of migration assistance services, including:
De‑racking
Transportation
Re‑racking
Optional re‑cabling and Smart Hands support
Providing structured migration support is expected to significantly increase customer uptake.
 
Information Required to Progress

Aptum requested that the customer tracking spreadsheet include the following information for each customer: Number of cabinets and approximate REUs
Power provisioned and, where available, estimated power utilisation
Power feeds (A/B), number of circuits
Connectivity details:
Port speeds (1G, 10G)
Bandwidth utilisation
Use of Centrilogic carriers vs. customer‑owned circuits
IP address information:
IP blocks assigned (e.g. /29)
Confirmation of inclusion within the proposed /24 block
Contact status:
Whether the customer has already been contacted
By whom and when

 

Connectivity and IP Considerations
Most customers rely on Centrilogic carrier mix (Telia, LINX, Cogent, Virgin, Vodafone).
A small number of customers use customer‑owned last‑mile circuits (primarily BT or Virgin).
No major technical blockers were identified:
Aptum has sufficient space and power capacity.
Carrier compatibility appears feasible.
Follow‑up is required to:
Confirm power requirements for the IBM mainframe equipment.
Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility for migrating customers.
Confirm treatment of the /24 IP block for customer continuity.
 
Customer Communications
Two draft customer notification letters have been prepared but have not yet been sent.
Formal communication is intentionally being held until landlord agreements are finalised.
It was agreed that:
Aptum should not contact customers directly until a formal introduction is made by Centrilogic.
The draft letter will be shared with Aptum for review (already shared).
Aptum will contribute language describing:
Facility capabilities
Migration assistance services
The previously referenced June 30 date is not yet firm and remains subject to landlord agreement.
 
Key Agreements
No critical technical blockers were identified for the majority of customers.
Migration assistance is essential to the success of customer transitions.
The enriched customer spreadsheet is the critical dependency for next steps.
Early and coordinated communication will be key once formal notices are issued.

Subject: Centrilogic & Aptum - Meeting Minutes – Bracknell update & Strategy - 26-03-30
 

Overview of Customer Footprint
The majority of customers are hosted in standard Centrilogic cabinets using shared power and carrier infrastructure.
One customer operates IBM mainframe equipment housed in non‑standard cabinets (wider and deeper), requiring confirmation of:
Power requirements
Weight and handling constraints
Only one customer (Perforce) operates within a dedicated cage (three cabinets plus expansion capacity).
Several customers also occupy office space in addition to data centre cabinets, which may influence their migration decisions.
 
Customer‑Specific Observations
Digital AI
Uses Centrilogic cabinets.
Requires physical separation between cabinets due to Wi‑Fi interference constraints.
Cloudian
Operates three cabinets with relatively high power consumption.
Also uses office space, though this is primarily for convenience.
It was agreed that, if migrating, the focus would likely be on rack space only, not office space.
Infinity
Uses one cabinet and office space.
Staff are onsite daily, making a migration to Portsmouth potentially less attractive.
OpenText
Uses six cabinets (five active, one for storage).
Makes frequent use of Smart Hands, which is included in their contract.
Perforce
Only cage customer at Bracknell.
Likely to seek an alternative location closer to their existing office, though requirements will be clarified in case migration is considered.
Telco providers (e.g., CityFibre, Colt)
Not expected to migrate to Aptum.
Will require time to remove or migrate their downstream customers from Bracknell.
 
Likelihood of Migration to Aptum
The general assessment is that more than 50% of customers may choose to migrate to Aptum.
A key differentiator will be the availability of migration assistance services, including:
De‑racking
Transportation
Re‑racking
Optional re‑cabling and Smart Hands support
Providing structured migration support is expected to significantly increase customer uptake.
 
Information Required to Progress

Aptum requested that the customer tracking spreadsheet include the following information for each customer: Number of cabinets and approximate REUs
Power provisioned and, where available, estimated power utilisation
Power feeds (A/B), number of circuits
Connectivity details:
Port speeds (1G, 10G)
Bandwidth utilisation
Use of Centrilogic carriers vs. customer‑owned circuits
IP address information:
IP blocks assigned (e.g. /29)
Confirmation of inclusion within the proposed /24 block
Contact status:
Whether the customer has already been contacted
By whom and when

 

Connectivity and IP Considerations
Most customers rely on Centrilogic carrier mix (Telia, LINX, Cogent, Virgin, Vodafone).
A small number of customers use customer‑owned last‑mile circuits (primarily BT or Virgin).
No major technical blockers were identified:
Aptum has sufficient space and power capacity.
Carrier compatibility appears feasible.
Follow‑up is required to:
Confirm power requirements for the IBM mainframe equipment.
Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility for migrating customers.
Confirm treatment of the /24 IP block for customer continuity.
 
Customer Communications
Two draft customer notification letters have been prepared but have not yet been sent.
Formal communication is intentionally being held until landlord agreements are finalised.
It was agreed that:
Aptum should not contact customers directly until a formal introduction is made by Centrilogic.
The draft letter will be shared with Aptum for review (already shared).
Aptum will contribute language describing:
Facility capabilities
Migration assistance services
The previously referenced June 30 date is not yet firm and remains subject to landlord agreement.
 
Key Agreements
No critical technical blockers were identified for the majority of customers.
Migration assistance is essential to the success of customer transitions.
The enriched customer spreadsheet is the critical dependency for next steps.
Early and coordinated communication will be key once formal notices are issued.

Cc: Martin Mossakowski <mmossakowski@centrilogic.com>
Subject: Centrilogic & Aptum - Meeting Minutes – Bracknell update & Strategy - 26-03-30
 

Overview of Customer Footprint
The majority of customers are hosted in standard Centrilogic cabinets using shared power and carrier infrastructure.
One customer operates IBM mainframe equipment housed in non‑standard cabinets (wider and deeper), requiring confirmation of:
Power requirements
Weight and handling constraints
Only one customer (Perforce) operates within a dedicated cage (three cabinets plus expansion capacity).
Several customers also occupy office space in addition to data centre cabinets, which may influence their migration decisions.
 
Customer‑Specific Observations
Digital AI
Uses Centrilogic cabinets.
Requires physical separation between cabinets due to Wi‑Fi interference constraints.
Cloudian
Operates three cabinets with relatively high power consumption.
Also uses office space, though this is primarily for convenience.
It was agreed that, if migrating, the focus would likely be on rack space only, not office space.
Infinity
Uses one cabinet and office space.
Staff are onsite daily, making a migration to Portsmouth potentially less attractive.
OpenText
Uses six cabinets (five active, one for storage).
Makes frequent use of Smart Hands, which is included in their contract.
Perforce
Only cage customer at Bracknell.
Likely to seek an alternative location closer to their existing office, though requirements will be clarified in case migration is considered.
Telco providers (e.g., CityFibre, Colt)
Not expected to migrate to Aptum.
Will require time to remove or migrate their downstream customers from Bracknell.
 
Likelihood of Migration to Aptum
The general assessment is that more than 50% of customers may choose to migrate to Aptum.
A key differentiator will be the availability of migration assistance services, including:
De‑racking
Transportation
Re‑racking
Optional re‑cabling and Smart Hands support
Providing structured migration support is expected to significantly increase customer uptake.
 
Information Required to Progress

Aptum requested that the customer tracking spreadsheet include the following information for each customer: Number of cabinets and approximate REUs
Power provisioned and, where available, estimated power utilisation
Power feeds (A/B), number of circuits
Connectivity details:
Port speeds (1G, 10G)
Bandwidth utilisation
Use of Centrilogic carriers vs. customer‑owned circuits
IP address information:
IP blocks assigned (e.g. /29)
Confirmation of inclusion within the proposed /24 block
Contact status:
Whether the customer has already been contacted
By whom and when

 

Connectivity and IP Considerations
Most customers rely on Centrilogic carrier mix (Telia, LINX, Cogent, Virgin, Vodafone).
A small number of customers use customer‑owned last‑mile circuits (primarily BT or Virgin).
No major technical blockers were identified:
Aptum has sufficient space and power capacity.
Carrier compatibility appears feasible.
Follow‑up is required to:
Confirm power requirements for the IBM mainframe equipment.
Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility for migrating customers.
Confirm treatment of the /24 IP block for customer continuity.
 
Customer Communications
Two draft customer notification letters have been prepared but have not yet been sent.
Formal communication is intentionally being held until landlord agreements are finalised.
It was agreed that:
Aptum should not contact customers directly until a formal introduction is made by Centrilogic.
The draft letter will be shared with Aptum for review (already shared).
Aptum will contribute language describing:
Facility capabilities
Migration assistance services
The previously referenced June 30 date is not yet firm and remains subject to landlord agreement.
 
Key Agreements
No critical technical blockers were identified for the majority of customers.
Migration assistance is essential to the success of customer transitions.
The enriched customer spreadsheet is the critical dependency for next steps.
Early and coordinated communication will be key once formal notices are issued.
 
 
Action Items
Sergio B
Update and enrich the customer spreadsheet. - ONGOING
Share the spreadsheet and draft customer letter in the agreed shared folder. DONE
Aptum
Review the draft customer communication.
Provide proposed language covering facility details and migration services. @Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com,  @george.revie@aptum.com  would it be possible to receive the revised version by end of day today (3/30)?
Network & Facilities
Confirm power requirements for IBM mainframe equipment.
Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility.
All parties
Re‑engage once landlord agreements allow formal customer communication.
 

Please feel free to add any comments or suggestions.
SERGIO BERNARDOU
Project Management Consultant
 
sbernardou@centrilogic.com
www.centrilogic.com

items.subject: Re: Inventory search and Data Center planning for Private Cloud.
items.body_text:
[Ian Rae]
We have the server inventory (Matt C), as well as a map of free space and power per DC (George. Will get the team on it and loop in Adam (cc) and Martin who has the current deployment plans.

Ian Rae
President & CEO
+1 (514) 944-4008
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: David Pistacchio (Consultant) <David.Pistacchio@digitalbridge.com>
Sent: Thursday, 09 April 2026 12:14:54
To: Ian Rae <ian.rae@aptum.com>; Sarah Blanchard <sblanchard@cloudops.com>
Subject: Inventory search and Data Center planning for Private Cloud. 
 

On our call on Tuesday, I believe that we agreed that we will need to look at all of our available inventory of equipment including what's deployed and no longer used, and what's not yet deployed, and determine what we will need to build a Private Cloud and Cloud MC environment in the UK and the US.  We also need to determine where we should set them up. 

It seems to me that we should get George and his team as well as the Logistics guys in Sarah's team working on this ASAP.  Is there any reason why we can't? 

Dave

items.subject: Sécuriser l’IA sans compromis
items.body_text:
[Fortinet]
De l'utilisation de GenAI aux charges de travail de l'IA, les priorités en matière de sécurité évoluent. 

Consulter l'e-mail en ligne

 

 

AMÉRIQUES 

21 avril 
9 h 00 (PDT) // 12 h 00 (EDT)

Inscriptions ouvertes 
La nouvelle réalité de la sécurité façonnée par l’IA 

Participez à notre événement virtuel pour découvrir ce qui change lorsque l’IA passe de l’expérimentation au déploiement opérationnel, et comment les organisations font évoluer leurs stratégies de sécurité à mesure que la surface d’attaque s’élargit.
Inscrivez-vous maintenant 

L’IA est rapidement passée d’un usage expérimental à une intégration dans les environnements d’entreprise, du GenAI aux charges de travail en production. À mesure que son adoption progresse, les équipes de sécurité et d’infrastructure doivent gérer une plus grande échelle, des flux de données plus complexes et de nouveaux risques, souvent sans pouvoir repenser leurs architectures existantes.

Lors du sommet, nous aborderons :

Comment l’IA remet en question les hypothèses de sécurité traditionnelles, de la visibilité et de la gouvernance jusqu’aux limites de confiance

Quelles décisions en matière de sécurité et d’infrastructure deviennent plus urgentes à mesure que les charges de travail liées à l’IA

Comment maintenir le contrôle sans ajouter d’outils fragmentés ni de complexité opérationnelle

Ce qu’il faut pour aligner sécurité, infrastructure et opérations autour d’une approche progressive de la sécurisation de l’IA

Si l’IA fait déjà partie de votre environnement, ce sommet vous aidera à mieux comprendre l’évolution des priorités en matière de sécurité.
​
​Joignez-vous au sommet virtuel dès aujourd’hui
Inscrivez-vous maintenant 

Sous-titrage disponible pendant l’événement.

items.subject: Welcome to your Grafana Partner Newsletter
items.body_text:
[Grafana Labs Team]
You don't often get email from partners@grafana.com. Learn why this is important 

Hello Grafana Labs Partners,
2025 was a truly standout year for Grafana Labs. We saw strong revenue growth, expanded adoption of AI across our platform, and continued leadership in open observability. None of this would have been possible without the strength of our partner ecosystem and the impact you continue to drive with us.
As we look ahead to the next fiscal year, we are evolving our Partner Program to ensure your business grows alongside our technology and momentum. Our updated program introduces a more targeted and high-value approach, with benefits centered on what matters most:
Simpler, more competitive margins
Margin for Partner-sourced net-new Grafana logos
And more enhancements designed to accelerate your growth
I will be sharing full details, including program requirements and new benefits, during an exclusive partner presentation on April 29. I encourage you to register and join us to learn how you can take full advantage of what’s ahead.
Register Now 
We’re excited about what we’re building together and look forward to continuing this journey with you.
Tom Kennedy
Global Head of Channels & Partnerships at Grafana LabsGrafana Labs and AWS Expand Strategic Collaboration to Accelerate Partner Growth
Grafana Labs has signed a new Strategic Collaboration Agreement (SCA) with AWS, strengthening our joint commitment to accelerate open observability adoption at scale.
This multi-year collaboration enhances how we co-build, co-sell, and co-market, making it easier for customers to adopt and scale Grafana Cloud on AWS through streamlined procurement, deeper integrations, and AI-driven innovation.
What this means for you:
More co-selling opportunities with AWS, including Marketplace-driven deals
Increased co-marketing support to drive joint demand and pipeline
Stronger technical alignment to help you deliver faster time to value for customers
This is a significant step forward in unlocking new growth opportunities across our shared ecosystem.
👉 Read the full announcement to learn how to take advantage of this expanded collaborationObservability Survey 2026: Thank you for your contribution
Our annual Observability Survey continues to be one of the most valuable ways we capture real-world insights from across the ecosystem, and this year is no exception.
A big thank you to all of our partners who contributed. Your input plays a critical role in shaping how we understand trends in observability, from AI adoption to evolving monitoring practices across industries.
We also appreciate the strong engagement with the Observability Survey Incentive. Many of you took advantage of the opportunity to participate and earn Grafana Swag Kits as a thank you for your time.
Winners will be notified directly soon.
👉 Explore the survey and see how your insights are helping shape the future of observabilityGrafanaCON 2026: The flagship event for the open observability community
Join us at GrafanaCON 2026, our largest global community event, taking place April 20–22 in Barcelona. This three-day experience brings together engineers, developers, and technology leaders to explore the latest innovations across Grafana and the open observability ecosystem.
From hands-on labs and deep technical sessions to real-world customer stories and live demos, GrafanaCON is where the community comes together to learn, share, and connect. Attendees will get a first look at what’s next for Grafana and the LGTM stack, while engaging directly with the teams and experts behind the technology.
For partners, GrafanaCON represents a unique opportunity to build relationships, showcase expertise, and connect with a highly engaged audience shaping the future of observability.
👉 Learn more and register to join us in BarcelonaPartner spotlight: DNX launches Datadog savings calculator
🚀 Kicking off the fiscal year with a strong start, DNX is bringing innovation to the forefront with their new Datadog savings calculator, now ready to be tested with customers.
Designed to help organizations evaluate potential cost efficiencies, the tool allows users to input their Datadog usage and explore savings opportunities.
👉 Curious to try it yourself? Test the calculator hereGrafana Labs Named to The Agentic List 2026
Grafana Labs has been recognized on The Agentic List 2026, highlighting the 120 most promising private companies building enterprise-grade agentic AI solutions. These companies are identified for their momentum over the past year and their growing impact across large enterprises and key industries.
We are especially proud to be included in the Safety, Alignment, and Observability category, reinforcing the critical role observability plays in building reliable and trustworthy AI systems.
Thank you to the AI Agent Conference for the recognition, and to our Grafanistas and community for your continued support.
👉 Check out the full list to see the companies shaping the future of agentic AIMeet Grafana Labs at Google Cloud Next 2026
Grafana Labs will be at Google Cloud Next 2026, taking place April 22–24 in Las Vegas, where you can explore how open observability and AI-powered insights come together in Grafana Cloud.
Visit us onsite to see live demos of the latest Grafana innovations, including full-stack observability, performance testing, and incident response, all unified across metrics, logs, traces, and profiles.
This is also a great opportunity to connect with our engineers, discover what’s new in open source, and learn how to monitor and optimize your Google Cloud environments with Grafana.
For partners, it’s a valuable moment to engage with customers, explore joint opportunities, and showcase how Grafana and Google Cloud together can drive deeper insights and faster outcomes.
👉 Stop by the Grafana booth or request a meeting to connect with the team in personConnect with Grafana Labs at AWS Summits
Grafana Labs is joining AWS Summits around the world, showcasing how AI-powered observability is transforming how teams monitor, troubleshoot, and optimize their systems.
Visit the Grafana booth to see how our latest innovations help teams move faster and operate more efficiently, including:
AI-driven incident response and management, enabling faster root cause analysis and guided remediation
Full-stack observability across metrics, logs, traces, and profiles for complete system visibility
Performance testing and optimization to proactively identify issues before they impact users
These sessions and demos highlight how Grafana Cloud helps organizations reduce complexity, improve reliability, and turn observability data into actionable insights.
For partners, AWS Summits are a great opportunity to engage customers around real-world use cases and position Grafana as a key component of modern, AI-enabled observability strategies.
👉 Find an AWS Summit near you and connect with the Grafana team onsiteWhy open source is the cheat code for AI
In this recent article, Grafana Labs CTO Tom Wilkie highlights why open source is becoming a critical advantage in the AI era. Rather than starting from scratch, organizations can build on widely adopted open tools, frameworks, and communities that AI models already understand, enabling faster innovation with fewer resources.
The piece emphasizes that open systems drive transparency, flexibility, and interoperability, helping teams avoid vendor lock-in while accelerating time to value. In a landscape where speed matters, open source provides what the author calls “instant AI readiness,” making it easier for teams to experiment, iterate, and scale.
For partners, this reinforces a key opportunity: leveraging open technologies not just to adopt AI, but to differentiate, build trust with customers, and deliver more adaptable solutions.
👉 Read the full article to explore why open source is emerging as a force multiplier for AI innovation
Monitoring AI agent infrastructure with Grafana Cloud
As AI agents become a critical part of modern systems, gaining visibility into their performance, reliability, and cost is essential. In this blog, Grafana Labs explores how to monitor Amazon Bedrock AgentCore infrastructure using Grafana Cloud, OpenTelemetry, and Amazon CloudWatch to achieve end-to-end observability.
The guide shows how teams can track key metrics such as latency, token usage, execution reliability, and error rates, all within a unified platform that brings together infrastructure and application insights.
By centralizing telemetry across multiple AWS accounts and regions, Grafana Cloud provides a single pane of glass for AI operations, helping teams troubleshoot faster, optimize performance, and better manage costs.
For partners, this highlights a growing opportunity to support customers in building and operating production-ready AI systems with full observability, ensuring reliability as AI adoption scales.
👉 Read the full blog to learn how to monitor and optimize AI agent infrastructure with Grafana CloudScaling and securing your logs
As cloud-native architectures continue to grow, managing log volume efficiently is more critical than ever. In this webinar, learn how to scale and secure your logging strategy with Grafana Loki, including practical approaches to reduce costs while maintaining performance and reliability.
👉 Watch the on-demand webinar to explore scalable, cost-effective logging with Grafana LokiCore observability education: Metrics fundamentals
For partners building or expanding their observability practice, this webinar covers the essentials of modern metrics. Learn how Prometheus, Grafana Mimir, and the broader Grafana stack work together to deliver scalable, high-performance metric storage and querying.
👉 Watch the on-demand webinar to strengthen your foundation in metrics and observabilityPerformance and user experience
Delivering great digital experiences requires visibility across both performance and user interactions. This session combines k6 load testing with frontend observability (Faro) to provide a complete view of application health, from backend systems to real user experience.
👉 Watch the on-demand webinar to test, monitor, and optimize performance with Grafana Cloud

This email was sent to anelson@cloudops.com. You may unsubscribe at any time.

The licensing problem involves the product key used to install SQL Server.  Microsoft gives the exact same product key to all Software Assurance (Enterprise)/MSDN (Developer)/SPLA (Hosting) customers.  Only retail product keys are unique.  Thus, in most cases, the product key is not proof of licensing.  In past audits, if Microsoft found this product key used for SQL Server, it assumed we provided the product key and thus licensing.  If we weren’t sending Microsoft their royalty for the license, we owed back licensing fees.
 
SQL Server + CAL is a retail-only product.  It is, to my knowledge, not offered under Software Assurance, nor can it offered under SPLA.
 
If a customer buys their own SQL Server licenses through Software Assurance, they have the ability to use these licenses in Azure, other public cloud, or a hosting provider.  This is done through License Mobility and requires the license holder to submit a form attesting that they have the required license.
 
Microsoft licensing is complex and my understanding is not complete.  However, since it carries a real financial liability if we get it wrong, I believe it’s important that we understand what Microsoft allows and requires.  I’ve documents that I’ve used to make sense of this.

Secondly, my understanding is that Charles may be incorrect (apologies Charles!), not sure if there was a recent change with the licensing models. My understanding is if this was a virtual server that can be moved between hosts, or is on a shared stack, or on our/their private cloud, or a newer version of SQL than what they are licensed for, the customer would 100% need SA on their licenses and would have to use the per core model based on the cores in the physical hosts. With Azure Arc or CSP licensing, you can now get away with just licensing the cores in the VM.
 
However, this is physical hardware dedicated to them with a Windows OS directly on top with presumably a fixed number of SQL connections, so they are allowed to use the Server license + CAL model, unless I'm missing something. @Charles was that information directly from an MS ticket? If not, we should probably open one to confirm if that model is allowed on a dedicated physical server in our data centers - I think it is now allowed.
 
In answer to the other questions, If their current licensing is confirmed as inadequate, when I compared the numbers SQL Arc would probably offer the cheapest SQL licensing for them as they can pay by the hour on their Azure consumption. [Expedient]
Chat on Nutanix, AI readiness, and infrastructure decisions that matter.️ 

Can't see this email? View it in your browser.

 

Expedient has been named Nutanix Service Provider of the Year for the second consecutive year. 
To keep that momentum going, join Anthony Jackman, Chief Innovation Officer at Expedient, and Jason Langone, Global AI Business Development at Nutanix, on Tuesday, April 28 at 10:30 AM ET for a practical coffee chat on the infrastructure decisions shaping what comes next. 
You’ll hear perspectives on: 
Where Nutanix can outperform hyperscale for steady-state workloads
​What many organizations miss when preparing infrastructure for AI
How Nutanix and Expedient’s AI CTRL Platform can help create an AI-ready foundation 
All attendees receive a coffee eGift card and will be entered to win a Breville Espresso Machine. 
​​​​​
Can't attend live? Register anyway to receive the full replay and key takeaways.

BREAKING NEWS: Ares Management Taking Whitestone REIT Private for $1.7 Billion 

 

Whitestone REIT and Ares Management Corporation said Thursday that Whitestone has entered into a definitive merger agreement with certain Ares Real Estate funds in which Ares will acquire all outstanding Whitestone common shares and operating partnership units in an all-cash transaction valued at approximately $1.7 billion. Houston-based Whitestone, which operates 56 convenience-focused retail properties across major markets in Texas and Arizona, announced in early March that it was exploring a sale.

items.subject: Navigating Today's Leadership Challenges: AI, Growth & Resilience
items.body_text:
[Amy Cutbill]
You don't often get email from cutbill@hortoninternational.com. Learn why this is important 

 
 
 

Strategic leadership trends shaping 2026
 

Hi Adam
 
As 2026 gathers pace, a set of consistent strategic themes is shaping leadership priorities, talent planning, and organisational performance. Drawing on our recent insights and market observations, several clear patterns are emerging.
 
Human-driven strategic planning
Leaders are moving beyond traditional business planning frameworks. High-performing organisations now integrate talent strategy, leadership succession, and workforce capability planning directly into core organisational planning rather than treating them as separate initiatives. This shift connects people investment to growth outcomes and long-term resilience.
 
Future-ready leadership capabilities
The executive leadership profile continues to evolve. Organisations are prioritising adaptability, strategic thinking, and human-centred leadership alongside operational competence. Modern leaders are expected to guide teams through complexity, transition, and uncertainty, not simply manage transactional objectives.
 
Balanced technology adoption
Digital tools and AI are accelerating change, but the real differentiator remains leadership that blends technology insight with genuine human understanding. Leaders who position technology as an enabler of people performance rather than a substitute for human judgement stand apart.
 
Leadership pipeline and talent development
Addressing leadership gaps early remains a priority. A proactive approach to developing future leaders and aligning talent acquisition with strategic goals ensures organisations are not reacting to change but actively shaping it.
 
If these trends are relevant to your leadership agenda this year, please feel free to get in touch.
 
Best regards,
The Horton International Team
 
Crafting Leadership, One Vision At A Time.
   

 
 

 

© 2025 Horton International, All rights reserved.
www.hortoninternational.com

items.body_text:
[notification@thinkon.com]
Hello,
Thank you for being a loyal ThinkOn customer. Our team of Thinkers are continuously working to deliver the seamless and positive digital performance experience you expect from ThinkOn.
Maintenance Details: vCenter upgrade to version 7.0.3w
Reason for Maintenance: Platform upgrade to maintain vendor support, enhance stability, and improve overall performance.
Start Time: April 23, 2026 at 2:00 AM BST
End Time: April 23, 2026 at 4:00 AM BST
Site(s) Impacted: LDN1
Services Affected: Access to the vCenter management interface may be intermittently unavailable during the maintenance window.
Service Impact: Virtual machine workloads will not be impacted. No changes will be made to existing access configurations during this window. Your data remains protected and secure at all times.
Workarounds / Support: As a workaround during the maintenance window, we recommend using your virtual pfSense firewall to establish VPN access to your environment to maintain management access to your virtual machines. Documentation can be found here: https://docs.netgate.com/pfsense/en/latest/vpn/index.html
Additionally, our Operations team will be available throughout the maintenance window to assist with any required changes. Our team will closely monitor all activities to ensure the maintenance is completed efficiently and with minimal disruption.
If you have any questions or concerns, please contact ThinkOn Support.
ThinkOn Operations and Customer Experience
Toll free: 1-877-787-0306
